metadata.description: Standard normal examination results for adolescents.

results.male_result: Inspection: Abdomen is flat and symmetric, no visible masses, distension, or pulsations; umbilicus is centrally located and inverted; no scars; physiological striae (stretch marks) associated with rapid growth may be present on the flanks or back; no visible peristalsis; no caput medusae or spider angiomata. Auscultation: Bowel sounds are normoactive (5-30 per minute), no bruits over aorta, renal, or iliac arteries. Percussion: Tympanic over gastric area and intestines; dull over liver and spleen; no shifting dullness; liver span 6-12 cm at right midclavicular line (correlated with body size). Palpation: Abdomen is soft and non-tender; no guarding, rigidity, or rebound tenderness; abdominal musculature may be well-developed; liver edge is smooth and non-palpable or palpable just below right costal margin; spleen is non-palpable; kidneys are non-palpable; no palpable masses; no inguinal hernias; no costovertebral angle tenderness.
results.female_result: Inspection: Abdomen is flat and symmetric, no visible masses, distension, or pulsations; umbilicus is centrally located and inverted; no scars; physiological striae (stretch marks) associated with rapid growth may be present on the flanks, hips, or back; no visible peristalsis; no caput medusae or spider angiomata. Auscultation: Bowel sounds are normoactive (5-30 per minute), no bruits over aorta, renal, or iliac arteries. Percussion: Tympanic over gastric area and intestines; dull over liver and spleen; no shifting dullness; liver span 6-12 cm at right midclavicular line (correlated with body size). Palpation: Abdomen is soft and non-tender; no guarding, rigidity, or rebound tenderness; liver edge is smooth and non-palpable or palpable just below right costal margin; spleen is non-palpable; kidneys are non-palpable; no palpable masses; uterus is non-palpable in non-pregnant state; no inguinal hernias; no costovertebral angle tenderness.

results.male_result: Not applicable - This is an emergency intervention technique (Heimlich maneuver) used to relieve foreign body airway obstruction, not a diagnostic examination. In healthy individuals, no airway obstruction is present, therefore this maneuver is not performed as a routine examination.
results.female_result: Not applicable - This is an emergency intervention technique (Heimlich maneuver) used to relieve foreign body airway obstruction, not a diagnostic examination. In healthy individuals, no airway obstruction is present, therefore this maneuver is not performed as a routine examination.

results.male_result: Negative. With the patient supine, knee flexed to 90 degrees, and foot stabilized, there is no excessive anterior translation of the tibia relative to the femur. The tibial displacement is less than 5mm with a firm endpoint. No pain or instability is elicited. The test is symmetric when compared to the contralateral knee.
results.female_result: Negative. With the patient supine, knee flexed to 90 degrees, and foot stabilized, there is no excessive anterior translation of the tibia relative to the femur. The tibial displacement is less than 5mm with a firm endpoint. No pain or instability is elicited. The test is symmetric when compared to the contralateral knee.

results.male_result: Nasal vestibule is intact with normal vibrissae. Nasal septum is midline without deviation, perforation, or ulceration. Inferior and middle turbinates are normal in size and color (pink, moist mucosa). Nasal mucosa appears pink, smooth, and well-hydrated without erythema, pallor, or edema. Nasal passages are patent bilaterally with no obstruction. No polyps, masses, crusting, or discharge observed. No bleeding or abnormal vessels noted.
results.female_result: Nasal vestibule is intact with normal vibrissae. Nasal septum is midline without deviation, perforation, or ulceration. Inferior and middle turbinates are normal in size and color (pink, moist mucosa). Nasal mucosa appears pink, smooth, and well-hydrated without erythema, pallor, or edema. Nasal passages are patent bilaterally with no obstruction. No polyps, masses, crusting, or discharge observed. No bleeding or abnormal vessels noted.

results.male_result: Height and weight are highly variable and dependent on age and pubertal stage (Tanner stage); results should be evaluated using age-specific growth charts (e.g., WHO or CDC percentiles). Normal range is typically between the 3rd and 97th percentiles. BMI is assessed by BMI-for-age percentiles: 5th percentile to <85th percentile is considered healthy weight. Head circumference reaches adult size (approx. 53-58 cm). Chest circumference and shoulder width increase significantly during the pubertal growth spurt (typically ages 12-16).
results.female_result: Height and weight are highly variable and dependent on age and pubertal stage; results should be evaluated using age-specific growth charts. Normal range is typically between the 3rd and 97th percentiles. BMI is assessed by BMI-for-age percentiles: 5th percentile to <85th percentile is considered healthy weight. The pubertal growth spurt typically occurs earlier than males (ages 10-14). Hip circumference increases due to pelvic widening, and body fat distribution changes significantly.

results.male_result: Not applicable (neonatal assessment only)
results.female_result: Not applicable (neonatal assessment only)

results.male_result: Negative - No palpable lymph nodes at the medial aspect of the inguinal ligament (Cloquet's node area). The region is soft, non-tender, with no discrete nodular masses detected on deep palpation.
results.female_result: Negative - No palpable lymph nodes at the medial aspect of the inguinal ligament (Cloquet's node area). The region is soft, non-tender, with no discrete nodular masses detected on deep palpation.

results.male_result: Negative. The test is performed with the patient seated or standing. The examiner abducts the arm to 90 degrees, extends the elbow, pronates the forearm, and flexes the wrist and fingers while laterally flexing the neck to the contralateral side. Normal finding: No reproduction of radicular pain or paresthesia along the upper extremity. Mild tension or stretching sensation in the arm is acceptable and considered within normal limits.
results.female_result: Negative. The test is performed with the patient seated or standing. The examiner abducts the arm to 90 degrees, extends the elbow, pronates the forearm, and flexes the wrist and fingers while laterally flexing the neck to the contralateral side. Normal finding: No reproduction of radicular pain or paresthesia along the upper extremity. Mild tension or stretching sensation in the arm is acceptable and considered within normal limits.

results.male_result: Chest wall is symmetric. Sexual maturity rating (Tanner staging) is consistent with age. Transient, distinct, firm, and often tender subareolar breast tissue (physiological gynecomastia) may be palpable, particularly during mid-puberty (Tanner stages 2-4), which is a common normal variant. No pathologic masses, nipple discharge, or axillary lymphadenopathy. Skin is intact.
results.female_result: Breast development is consistent with the appropriate Tanner stage (1-5). Mild asymmetry in breast size is a common physiological finding during development. Breast tissue is soft; generalized nodularity or tenderness may be present depending on the menstrual cycle. No discrete dominant masses, skin dimpling, retraction, or nipple discharge. No axillary lymphadenopathy.

results.male_result: Negative: Less than 10 petechiae per 5 cm diameter circle (approximately 2.5 cm radius) on the volar surface of the forearm after 5 minutes of venous occlusion at a pressure midway between systolic and diastolic blood pressure. Skin appears normal without ecchymoses or purpura.
results.female_result: Negative: Less than 10 petechiae per 5 cm diameter circle (approximately 2.5 cm radius) on the volar surface of the forearm after 5 minutes of venous occlusion at a pressure midway between systolic and diastolic blood pressure. Skin appears normal without ecchymoses or purpura. Note: Females may occasionally show slightly higher petechiae counts (up to 10-20) which may still be considered within normal limits.

results.male_result: 4-7 cm (Values increase with pubertal growth; early adolescents may range 4-5 cm, while late adolescents typically achieve adult values ≥5 cm)
results.female_result: 3-5 cm (Generally consistent with adult values; measurement technique requires adjustment for developing breast tissue)

results.male_result: Visual acuity: 20/20 (6/6) or better in each eye with or without correction. Pupils: Equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA), 2-6 mm in diameter. Extraocular movements: Intact in all six cardinal directions without nystagmus or strabismus. Visual fields: Full to confrontation testing. Intraocular pressure: 10-21 mmHg. External examination: Eyelids symmetrical without ptosis, edema, or lesions; conjunctivae clear and non-injected; sclerae white and anicteric. Anterior segment: Cornea clear and transparent; anterior chamber deep and quiet; iris pattern normal. Lens: Clear without opacities. Fundoscopic examination: Optic disc margins sharp and well-defined with cup-to-disc ratio 0.3 or less; retinal vessels with normal arteriovenous ratio (2:3); macula with normal foveal light reflex; retina flat and without hemorrhages, exudates, or detachment. Color vision: Normal trichromatic vision.
results.female_result: Visual acuity: 20/20 (6/6) or better in each eye with or without correction. Pupils: Equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA), 2-6 mm in diameter. Extraocular movements: Intact in all six cardinal directions without nystagmus or strabismus. Visual fields: Full to confrontation testing. Intraocular pressure: 10-21 mmHg. External examination: Eyelids symmetrical without ptosis, edema, or lesions; conjunctivae clear and non-injected; sclerae white and anicteric. Anterior segment: Cornea clear and transparent; anterior chamber deep and quiet; iris pattern normal. Lens: Clear without opacities. Fundoscopic examination: Optic disc margins sharp and well-defined with cup-to-disc ratio 0.3 or less; retinal vessels with normal arteriovenous ratio (2:3); macula with normal foveal light reflex; retina flat and without hemorrhages, exudates, or detachment. Color vision: Normal trichromatic vision.

results.male_result: Cornea is clear and transparent with smooth surface. No opacities, ulcerations, or vascularization present. Corneal reflex is intact and brisk. Corneal sensitivity is normal. No arcus senilis in young adults. Corneal curvature is regular. No edema, infiltrates, or foreign bodies observed. Tear film is evenly distributed.
results.female_result: Cornea is clear and transparent with smooth surface. No opacities, ulcerations, or vascularization present. Corneal reflex is intact and brisk. Corneal sensitivity is normal. No arcus senilis in young adults. Corneal curvature is regular. No edema, infiltrates, or foreign bodies observed. Tear film is evenly distributed.

results.male_result: No movement of either eye observed during cover-uncover test; eyes maintain steady fixation with orthophoria (no latent or manifest strabismus); no refixation movement when cover is removed; binocular alignment is maintained in all cardinal positions of gaze
results.female_result: No movement of either eye observed during cover-uncover test; eyes maintain steady fixation with orthophoria (no latent or manifest strabismus); no refixation movement when cover is removed; binocular alignment is maintained in all cardinal positions of gaze

results.male_result: Normal skin architecture; pigmented nevi frequently exhibit a globular pattern (indicating benign growth) or a regular reticular network; lesions are symmetrical with uniform pigment distribution; absence of eccentric structureless areas or irregular streaks. Signs of increased sebaceous activity (e.g., open comedones, enlarged follicles) may be present due to puberty. No signs of ulceration or atypical vascular patterns.
results.female_result: Normal skin architecture; pigmented nevi frequently exhibit a globular pattern (indicating benign growth) or a regular reticular network; lesions are symmetrical with uniform pigment distribution; absence of eccentric structureless areas or irregular streaks. Signs of increased sebaceous activity (e.g., open comedones, enlarged follicles) may be present due to puberty. No signs of ulceration or atypical vascular patterns.

results.male_result: Anal sphincter tone is normal. Rectal mucosa is smooth without palpable masses, nodules, or tenderness. The prostate gland is palpable but generally smaller than in adults (size varies according to Tanner stage of pubertal development), smooth, symmetrical, non-tender, with a rubbery consistency. No blood or abnormal discharge on examining finger upon withdrawal.
results.female_result: Anal sphincter tone is normal. Rectal mucosa is smooth without palpable masses, nodules, or tenderness. The cervix may be palpable anteriorly through the rectal wall and is non-tender. Rectovaginal septum is intact without masses. No blood or abnormal discharge on examining finger upon withdrawal.

results.male_result: Negative test indicated by a firm end-feel with no excessive medial joint line opening or medial clear space widening; no pain, laxity, or clunking sensation observed compared to the contralateral side.
results.female_result: Negative test indicated by a firm end-feel with no excessive medial joint line opening or medial clear space widening; no pain, laxity, or clunking sensation observed compared to the contralateral side.

results.male_result: Values increase with age due to orbital growth. Early adolescence (approx. 10-14 years): 10-18 mm; Late adolescence (15-19 years): approaches adult range (12-21 mm). Difference between both eyes ≤2 mm. Eyes are symmetrically positioned.
results.female_result: Values increase with age due to orbital growth. Early adolescence (approx. 10-14 years): 10-18 mm; Late adolescence (15-19 years): approaches adult range (12-20 mm). Difference between both eyes ≤2 mm. Eyes are symmetrically positioned.

results.male_result: Eyelids: No ptosis, edema, or lesions; normal position and movement. Conjunctiva: Clear, no injection, discharge, or foreign bodies; palpebral and bulbar conjunctiva pink and moist. Sclera: White, no icterus or injection. Cornea: Clear and transparent, no opacities, ulcers, or abrasions; smooth light reflex. Pupils: Equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA), 3-5 mm in diameter in standard room light. Iris: Normal color and pattern, no synechiae. Lacrimal apparatus: No excessive tearing or discharge; puncta patent. Extraocular movements: Full range of motion in all six cardinal directions, no nystagmus or strabismus. Orbital structures: No proptosis or enophthalmos; symmetrical appearance.
results.female_result: Eyelids: No ptosis, edema, or lesions; normal position and movement. Conjunctiva: Clear, no injection, discharge, or foreign bodies; palpebral and bulbar conjunctiva pink and moist. Sclera: White, no icterus or injection. Cornea: Clear and transparent, no opacities, ulcers, or abrasions; smooth light reflex. Pupils: Equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA), 3-5 mm in diameter in standard room light. Iris: Normal color and pattern, no synechiae. Lacrimal apparatus: No excessive tearing or discharge; puncta patent. Extraocular movements: Full range of motion in all six cardinal directions, no nystagmus or strabismus. Orbital structures: No proptosis or enophthalmos; symmetrical appearance.

results.male_result: Genital development consistent with chronological age and Tanner staging (Stages I-V). Penis: Size and thickness appropriate for pubertal stage; foreskin may be retractable or exhibit physiological tightness in early adolescence. Testes: Both descended, palpable in scrotum; testicular volume appropriate for Tanner stage (increasing from <4ml in prepuberty to 15-25ml in adulthood). Scrotum: Skin texture (rugations) and pigmentation changes consistent with pubertal development. Pubic hair: Density, texture, and distribution consistent with Tanner staging. No masses, hernias, or abnormal discharge.
results.female_result: Genital development consistent with chronological age and Tanner staging (Stages I-V). Vulva: Labia majora and minora development appropriate for pubertal stage. Pubic hair: Density, texture, and distribution consistent with Tanner staging. Clitoris: Normal size, no hypertrophy. Introitus: Hymen typically intact (annular, crescentic, or fimbriated) in sexually naive adolescents. Mucosa: Moist and pink; physiologic leukorrhea (non-purulent, whitish discharge) is common during puberty. No erythema, edema, lesions, or abnormal discharge.

results.male_result: Eyelids are symmetrical bilaterally with normal position and contour. No ptosis, ectropion, or entropion present. Eyelid margins are smooth and well-apposed to the globe. No edema, erythema, or swelling observed. Eyelashes are present and properly directed outward. No lesions, masses, or nodules palpable. Palpebral fissures are equal in width bilaterally (approximately 8-11 mm). Lid closure is complete and symmetric. No lagophthalmos present. Meibomian gland orifices are patent without obstruction.
results.female_result: Eyelids are symmetrical bilaterally with normal position and contour. No ptosis, ectropion, or entropion present. Eyelid margins are smooth and well-apposed to the globe. No edema, erythema, or swelling observed. Eyelashes are present and properly directed outward. No lesions, masses, or nodules palpable. Palpebral fissures are equal in width bilaterally (approximately 8-11 mm). Lid closure is complete and symmetric. No lagophthalmos present. Meibomian gland orifices are patent without obstruction.

results.male_result: Negative. Patient lies prone with knee flexed to 90 degrees. Examiner extends the hip by lifting the thigh off the examination table. No pain or discomfort is elicited in the anterior thigh or groin region. No radiation of pain along the L2-L4 dermatome distribution. The test does not reproduce any neurological symptoms.
results.female_result: Negative. Patient lies prone with knee flexed to 90 degrees. Examiner extends the hip by lifting the thigh off the examination table. No pain or discomfort is elicited in the anterior thigh or groin region. No radiation of pain along the L2-L4 dermatome distribution. The test does not reproduce any neurological symptoms.

results.male_result: Not applicable
results.female_result: Score < 8 (normal); Score 8-15 (mild hirsutism); Score ≥ 15 (moderate to severe hirsutism). Normal healthy adult females typically score 0-7 points.

results.male_result: Not applicable
results.female_result: Not applicable (examination only performed during pregnancy; normal fetal heart rate in pregnant women: 110-160 beats per minute, regular rhythm, best heard at the fetal back position)

results.male_result: 0-15 cm; While touching the floor is ideal, adolescents (especially during rapid growth phases) may exhibit reduced flexibility, making distances up to 15 cm acceptable within normal limits.
results.female_result: 0-10 cm; Females typically retain greater flexibility than males, but may still experience slight regression during growth spurts. Touching the floor or reaching within 10 cm is considered normal.

results.male_result: Open angle configuration (Grade III-IV by Shaffer classification). All angle structures are clearly visible: ciliary body band, scleral spur, trabecular meshwork (typically exhibiting minimal to no pigmentation in adolescents), and Schwalbe's line. No peripheral anterior synechiae (PAS). No abnormal pigmentation, iris processes, or neovascularization. Angle width is approximately 35-45 degrees.
results.female_result: Open angle configuration (Grade III-IV by Shaffer classification). All angle structures are clearly visible: ciliary body band, scleral spur, trabecular meshwork (typically exhibiting minimal to no pigmentation in adolescents), and Schwalbe's line. No peripheral anterior synechiae (PAS). No abnormal pigmentation, iris processes, or neovascularization. Angle width is approximately 35-45 degrees.

results.male_result: Normal range of motion: 45-50 degrees of abduction. The test is performed with the patient supine, hip in neutral position. Movement is smooth and painless throughout the full range. Adolescents generally possess greater ligamentous laxity than adults, resulting in slightly higher mobility, though temporary tightness in adductor muscles may occur during rapid growth spurts.
results.female_result: Normal range of motion: 45-55 degrees of abduction. The test is performed with the patient supine, hip in neutral position. Movement is smooth and painless throughout the full range. Adolescent females typically demonstrate greater hip flexibility due to pelvic anatomy and hormonal factors affecting ligament laxity.

results.male_result: Nasopharyngeal mucosa is pink and moist with smooth surface. No erythema, edema, or ulceration. Adenoid tissue is typically undergoing physiological involution (regression) or is minimal; no significant hypertrophy or obstruction of the posterior choanae is observed. Eustachian tube orifices are patent and symmetric bilaterally. Fossa of Rosenmüller appears normal without masses or asymmetry. No abnormal secretions, polyps, or lesions observed. Posterior nasal septum is midline.
results.female_result: Nasopharyngeal mucosa is pink and moist with smooth surface. No erythema, edema, or ulceration. Adenoid tissue is typically undergoing physiological involution (regression) or is minimal; no significant hypertrophy or obstruction of the posterior choanae is observed. Eustachian tube orifices are patent and symmetric bilaterally. Fossa of Rosenmüller appears normal without masses or asymmetry. No abnormal secretions, polyps, or lesions observed. Posterior nasal septum is midline.

results.male_result: Mental status: Alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation; appropriate affect and behavior. Cranial nerves I-XII: Intact bilaterally; pupils equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA); extraocular movements intact without nystagmus; facial sensation and motor function symmetric; hearing grossly intact; palate elevates symmetrically; tongue midline without fasciculations; sternocleidomastoid and trapezius strength normal. Motor: Normal muscle bulk and tone in all extremities; strength 5/5 throughout; no pronator drift; no tremor or involuntary movements. Sensory: Light touch, pinprick, vibration, and proprioception intact in all dermatomes bilaterally. Reflexes: Deep tendon reflexes 2+ and symmetric (biceps, triceps, brachioradialis, patellar, Achilles); plantar responses flexor bilaterally (Babinski negative). Coordination: Finger-to-nose and heel-to-shin testing normal; rapid alternating movements intact; no dysmetria or dysdiadochokinesia. Gait: Normal stance and gait; tandem walking intact; Romberg test negative.
results.female_result: Mental status: Alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation; appropriate affect and behavior. Cranial nerves I-XII: Intact bilaterally; pupils equal, round, reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA); extraocular movements intact without nystagmus; facial sensation and motor function symmetric; hearing grossly intact; palate elevates symmetrically; tongue midline without fasciculations; sternocleidomastoid and trapezius strength normal. Motor: Normal muscle bulk and tone in all extremities; strength 5/5 throughout; no pronator drift; no tremor or involuntary movements. Sensory: Light touch, pinprick, vibration, and proprioception intact in all dermatomes bilaterally. Reflexes: Deep tendon reflexes 2+ and symmetric (biceps, triceps, brachioradialis, patellar, Achilles); plantar responses flexor bilaterally (Babinski negative). Coordination: Finger-to-nose and heel-to-shin testing normal; rapid alternating movements intact; no dysmetria or dysdiadochokinesia. Gait: Normal stance and gait; tandem walking intact; Romberg test negative.

results.male_result: 0 cm (occiput touches the wall with ease while standing with heels and back against the wall, chin held level, eyes looking straight ahead; no gap between the occiput and wall surface)
results.female_result: 0 cm (occiput touches the wall with ease while standing with heels and back against the wall, chin held level, eyes looking straight ahead; no gap between the occiput and wall surface)

results.male_result: Full extraocular movements in all six cardinal directions of gaze (up, down, left, right, up-left, up-right, down-left, down-right). No nystagmus observed. Conjugate eye movements present. No strabismus or diplopia. Smooth pursuit and saccadic movements are intact. Cover-uncover test reveals no deviation. Convergence is normal with near point of convergence at 8-10 cm or less.
results.female_result: Full extraocular movements in all six cardinal directions of gaze (up, down, left, right, up-left, up-right, down-left, down-right). No nystagmus observed. Conjugate eye movements present. No strabismus or diplopia. Smooth pursuit and saccadic movements are intact. Cover-uncover test reveals no deviation. Convergence is normal with near point of convergence at 8-10 cm or less.

results.male_result: Red reflex present and symmetric bilaterally. Optic disc appears round or slightly oval with sharp, well-defined margins. Cup-to-disc ratio approximately 0.3 or less. Disc color is pink-orange with normal physiologic pallor temporally. Retinal vessels show normal arteriovenous ratio of 2:3 to 3:4. Arteries appear bright red with central light reflex; veins appear darker red without pulsations (or with spontaneous venous pulsations at disc). No arteriovenous nicking, hemorrhages, exudates, or microaneurysms. Macula appears darker than surrounding retina with intact foveal light reflex. Retinal background is uniform orange-red without pigmentary changes, detachment, or lesions.
results.female_result: Same

results.male_result: Lips: Pink, moist, no cyanosis, no lesions or fissures. Oral mucosa: Pink, moist, intact, no ulcerations, no leukoplakia. Gingiva: Pink, firm, no swelling, bleeding, or recession. Teeth: Dentition appropriate for developmental stage; mixed dentition (primary and permanent teeth) may be present in early adolescence (ages 10-12); full permanent dentition (28 teeth) typically present by age 13; third molars typically unerupted or erupting in late adolescence; no visible caries. Tongue: Pink, moist, normal size and mobility, no coating abnormalities. Hard palate: Intact, pink, no lesions. Soft palate: Symmetric elevation on phonation. Uvula: Midline, elevates symmetrically. Tonsils: Grade 1-2, no erythema or exudates. Pharynx: Pink, no erythema. Salivary gland ducts: Patent. Breath: No halitosis.
results.female_result: Lips: Pink, moist, no cyanosis, no lesions or fissures. Oral mucosa: Pink, moist, intact, no ulcerations, no leukoplakia. Gingiva: Pink, firm, no swelling, bleeding, or recession. Teeth: Dentition appropriate for developmental stage; mixed dentition (primary and permanent teeth) may be present in early adolescence (ages 10-12); full permanent dentition (28 teeth) typically present by age 13; third molars typically unerupted or erupting in late adolescence; no visible caries. Tongue: Pink, moist, normal size and mobility, no coating abnormalities. Hard palate: Intact, pink, no lesions. Soft palate: Symmetric elevation on phonation. Uvula: Midline, elevates symmetrically. Tonsils: Grade 1-2, no erythema or exudates. Pharynx: Pink, no erythema. Salivary gland ducts: Patent. Breath: No halitosis.

results.male_result: Oral mucosa is pink, moist, and intact without lesions or ulcerations. Permanent dentition is generally complete (excluding third molars) with healthy gingiva; orthodontic appliances may be present. Tongue is midline, pink, moist, with normal papillae and no coating or lesions; tongue movement is symmetric and unrestricted. Hard and soft palate are intact with normal coloration. Uvula is midline and elevates symmetrically with phonation. Tonsils are present (or surgically absent), symmetric, and may be slightly more prominent than in adults (Grade 1-2), but without exudate, erythema, or signs of acute inflammation. Posterior pharyngeal wall is pink without erythema, exudate, or masses. Gag reflex is intact bilaterally. No halitosis noted.
results.female_result: Oral mucosa is pink, moist, and intact without lesions or ulcerations. Permanent dentition is generally complete (excluding third molars) with healthy gingiva; orthodontic appliances may be present. Tongue is midline, pink, moist, with normal papillae and no coating or lesions; tongue movement is symmetric and unrestricted. Hard and soft palate are intact with normal coloration. Uvula is midline and elevates symmetrically with phonation. Tonsils are present (or surgically absent), symmetric, and may be slightly more prominent than in adults (Grade 1-2), but without exudate, erythema, or signs of acute inflammation. Posterior pharyngeal wall is pink without erythema, exudate, or masses. Gag reflex is intact bilaterally. No halitosis noted.

results.male_result: Supine: Blood pressure approximately 100-130/60-85 mmHg (dependent on age and height percentiles), Heart rate 60-100 bpm. Standing (after 1-3 minutes): Blood pressure decrease <20 mmHg systolic and <10 mmHg diastolic, Heart rate increase <40 bpm. No symptoms of dizziness, lightheadedness, or syncope upon position change. Normal compensatory response.
results.female_result: Supine: Blood pressure approximately 100-120/60-80 mmHg (dependent on age and height percentiles), Heart rate 60-100 bpm. Standing (after 1-3 minutes): Blood pressure decrease <20 mmHg systolic and <10 mmHg diastolic, Heart rate increase <40 bpm. No symptoms of dizziness, lightheadedness, or syncope upon position change. Normal compensatory response.

results.male_result: External auditory canal is patent and free of obstruction, cerumen minimal or absent, canal skin intact without erythema or edema. Tympanic membrane is intact, pearly gray in color, translucent, with visible cone of light reflex at 5 o'clock position (right ear) and 7 o'clock position (left ear). Malleus handle and umbo are clearly visible through the membrane. No perforation, retraction, bulging, or fluid level behind the tympanic membrane. No signs of infection, scarring, or cholesteatoma.
results.female_result: External auditory canal is patent and free of obstruction, cerumen minimal or absent, canal skin intact without erythema or edema. Tympanic membrane is intact, pearly gray in color, translucent, with visible cone of light reflex at 5 o'clock position (right ear) and 7 o'clock position (left ear). Malleus handle and umbo are clearly visible through the membrane. No perforation, retraction, bulging, or fluid level behind the tympanic membrane. No signs of infection, scarring, or cholesteatoma.

results.male_result: Negative. With the patient supine and knee extended, the examiner applies downward pressure on the superior pole of the patella while the patient contracts the quadriceps muscle. Normal finding: No pain or crepitus is elicited. The patella glides smoothly under compression without discomfort. The patient can maintain quadriceps contraction against resistance without pain.
results.female_result: Negative. With the patient supine and knee extended, the examiner applies downward pressure on the superior pole of the patella while the patient contracts the quadriceps muscle. Normal finding: No pain or crepitus is elicited. The patella glides smoothly under compression without discomfort. The patient can maintain quadriceps contraction against resistance without pain.

results.male_result: Negative. With the patient supine and knee extended, compression of the suprapatellar pouch followed by a sharp downward tap on the patella produces no palpable click or floating sensation. The patella remains firmly seated against the femoral condyles with no ballottement detected, indicating absence of significant knee joint effusion.
results.female_result: Negative. With the patient supine and knee extended, compression of the suprapatellar pouch followed by a sharp downward tap on the patella produces no palpable click or floating sensation. The patella remains firmly seated against the femoral condyles with no ballottement detected, indicating absence of significant knee joint effusion.

results.male_result: Negative. The patient lies supine with the tested hip flexed, abducted, and externally rotated, placing the lateral malleolus on the contralateral knee. Gentle downward pressure on the flexed knee allows the thigh to fall freely toward the examination table without pain or restriction. No pain is elicited in the hip joint, sacroiliac joint, or groin region. Full range of motion is achieved with the knee approaching or reaching the level of the examination table.
results.female_result: Negative. The patient lies supine with the tested hip flexed, abducted, and externally rotated, placing the lateral malleolus on the contralateral knee. Gentle downward pressure on the flexed knee allows the thigh to fall freely toward the examination table without pain or restriction. No pain is elicited in the hip joint, sacroiliac joint, or groin region. Full range of motion is achieved with the knee approaching or reaching the level of the examination table.

results.male_result: Negative. No pain or discomfort elicited upon bilateral compression of the iliac crests toward midline (compression test) or upon outward pressure on the anterior superior iliac spines (distraction test). results.female_result: Negative. results.male_result: Not applicable (pelvic examination is a gynecological examination specific to females; male equivalent would be genitourinary examination assessing Tanner staging of external genitalia).
results.female_result: External genitalia: Appearance consistent with sexual maturity rating (Tanner staging 1-5) regarding pubic hair and labial development; hymen anatomy varies (annular, septate, etc.) and is typically intact in non-sexually active adolescents. Vagina: In post-pubertal adolescents, mucosa is estrogenized (pink, moist, rugated); physiological leukorrhea (non-odorous, mucoid, clear-to-white discharge) is a normal finding around menarche. Cervix: Smooth, pink; cervical ectopy (presence of glandular epithelium on the ectocervix appearing red) is a common physiological finding in adolescents and is not pathological. Bimanual examination (only if indicated): Uterus size varies from small/tubular (pre-pubertal) to adult pear-shape (post-pubertal); ovaries are generally non-palpable.

results.male_result: Penile dimensions correlate with sexual maturity rating (Tanner Stages). Size increases progressively from pre-pubertal dimensions to adult size. Evaluation depends on Tanner staging: Stage 1 (pre-pubertal size), Stage 2 (slight enlargement), Stage 3 (lengthening), Stage 4 (increase in breadth and glans development), and Stage 5 (adult size). By late adolescence (typically 16-19 years), measurements align with adult ranges (Flaccid: 7-10 cm; Stretched: 12-16 cm). Anatomy should be normal; foreskin should be retractable in older adolescents unless circumcised, and the urethral meatus should be at the tip of the glans.
results.female_result: Not applicable

results.female_result: Probing depth: 1-3 mm at all sites of fully erupted teeth; No bleeding on probing (BOP); No suppuration; Firm, pink gingiva with knife-edge margins; No clinical attachment loss; Tight adaptation of gingival tissues to tooth surfaces.

results.male_result: General appearance: Alert, oriented, well-nourished, pubertal development appropriate for age, no acute distress. Vital signs: Blood pressure varies by age, height, and gender (typically <120/80 mmHg), Heart rate 60-100 bpm, Respiratory rate 12-20 breaths/min, Temperature 36.1-37.2°C. Height and weight: BMI for age between 5th and 85th percentile (growth charts used). Head: Normocephalic. Eyes: PERRLA, conjunctivae pink. Ears: TM intact. Nose: Mucosa pink. Throat: Oropharynx clear. Neck: Supple, no lymphadenopathy, thyroid non-enlarged. Chest: Clear to auscultation, symmetric expansion. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, no organomegaly. Musculoskeletal: Full range of motion, normal strength, spine straight, negative Adams forward bend test (no evidence of scoliosis). Neurological: Intact. Skin: Warm, dry, may show evidence of acne vulgaris (comedones/papules) common in this age group. Genitourinary: Sexual Maturity Rating (Tanner Staging) of pubic hair and genitalia appropriate for age, testes descended.
results.female_result: General appearance: Alert, oriented, well-nourished, pubertal development appropriate for age, no acute distress. Vital signs: Blood pressure varies by age, height, and gender (typically <120/80 mmHg), Heart rate 60-100 bpm, Respiratory rate 12-20 breaths/min, Temperature 36.1-37.2°C. Height and weight: BMI for age between 5th and 85th percentile (growth charts used). Head: Normocephalic. Eyes: PERRLA. Ears: TM intact. Nose: Mucosa pink. Throat: Oropharynx clear. results.male_result: External auditory canal is patent and free of obstruction, cerumen, or discharge. Tympanic membrane is intact, translucent, and pearly gray in color with visible cone of light reflex in the anteroinferior quadrant. Malleus handle and umbo are clearly visible. Upon insufflation, the tympanic membrane demonstrates normal mobility with brisk movement both medially (positive pressure) and laterally (negative pressure), indicating normal middle ear pressure and Eustachian tube function. No retraction, bulging, perforation, or effusion noted.
results.female_result: Same

results.male_result: Abdomen is soft, non-tender, and non-distended with no palpable masses. No tenderness or rebound tenderness. No hepatosplenomegaly (liver and spleen are not palpable below the costal margin; however, the liver edge may be felt in thin adolescents during deep inspiration). Kidneys are not palpable. Superficial lymph nodes are generally not palpable, though small, mobile, non-tender 'shotty' nodes are often considered normal. Thyroid gland is not enlarged, has a soft texture, and no nodules are palpable. Tactile fremitus is symmetric bilaterally over the lung fields. Peripheral pulses are palpable, strong, and symmetric.
results.female_result: Abdomen is soft, non-tender, and non-distended with no palpable masses. No tenderness or rebound tenderness. No hepatosplenomegaly (liver and spleen are not palpable below the costal margin; however, the liver edge may be felt in thin adolescents during deep inspiration). Kidneys are not palpable. Superficial lymph nodes are generally not palpable, though small, mobile, non-tender 'shotty' nodes are often considered normal. Thyroid gland is not enlarged, has a soft texture, and no nodules are palpable. Tactile fremitus is symmetric bilaterally over the lung fields. Peripheral pulses are palpable, strong, and symmetric.

The test indicates no tension or irritation of the femoral nerve or upper lumbar nerve roots (L2-L4).

results.male_result: Normal postural response: Patient maintains balance with no more than 1-2 steps backward to recover equilibrium. No retropulsion observed. Upright posture is quickly restored without assistance. Protective stepping reflexes are intact and appropriate.
results.female_result: Normal postural response: Patient maintains balance with no more than 1-2 steps backward to recover equilibrium. No retropulsion observed. Upright posture is quickly restored without assistance. Protective stepping reflexes are intact and appropriate.

results.male_result: Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA). Direct light reflex: brisk constriction of the ipsilateral pupil upon light stimulation. Consensual light reflex: simultaneous constriction of the contralateral pupil. Pupil size typically ranges from 3-5 mm in diameter in ambient light. No afferent pupillary defect (Marcus Gunn pupil) detected. Symmetric response bilaterally with prompt constriction and appropriate dilation upon light removal.
results.female_result: Pupils are equal, round, and reactive to light and accommodation (PERRLA). Direct light reflex: brisk constriction of the ipsilateral pupil upon light stimulation. Consensual light reflex: simultaneous constriction of the contralateral pupil. Pupil size typically ranges from 3-5 mm in diameter in ambient light. No afferent pupillary defect (Marcus Gunn pupil) detected. Symmetric response bilaterally with prompt constriction and appropriate dilation upon light removal.

results.male_result: Bilateral red reflexes are present, symmetric, and bright. The reflex appears as a homogeneous reddish-orange glow emanating from the pupil when illuminated with a direct ophthalmoscope at approximately 12-18 inches distance. No dark spots, white reflexes (leukocoria), or asymmetry observed. The color may range from red to orange depending on fundus pigmentation.
results.female_result: Bilateral red reflexes are present, symmetric, and bright. The reflex appears as a homogeneous reddish-orange glow emanating from the pupil when illuminated with a direct ophthalmoscope at approximately 12-18 inches distance. No dark spots, white reflexes (leukocoria), or asymmetry observed. The color may range from red to orange depending on fundus pigmentation.

results.male_result: ≥5 cm increase in distance between marks upon maximal forward flexion. Starting position: two marks placed 10 cm apart (one at the lumbosacral junction at the level of L5-S1, and one 10 cm above). Normal lumbar spine flexion results in the distance between marks increasing to at least 15 cm (≥5 cm expansion).
results.female_result: ≥5 cm increase in distance between marks upon maximal forward flexion. Starting position: two marks placed 10 cm apart (one at the lumbosacral junction at the level of L5-S1, and one 10 cm above). results.male_result: Able to perform a controlled single-leg squat to approximately 60 degrees of knee flexion while maintaining balance. The trunk remains upright without excessive lateral lean. The knee tracks over the second toe without significant inward collapse (dynamic valgus). Pelvis remains relatively level without a significant drop on the non-standing side. Movement is smooth and controlled, indicating age-appropriate neuromuscular control and hip/knee stability.
results.female_result: Able to perform a controlled single-leg squat to approximately 60 degrees of knee flexion while maintaining balance. The trunk remains upright. While dynamic knee valgus is more common in adolescent females due to pelvic widening, a normal result requires the knee to track generally over the foot without excessive medial deviation. The pelvis remains level (negative Trendelenburg). Execution should be smooth and pain-free, showing adequate core and hip abductor strength.

results.male_result: Skin is warm, dry, and intact with good turgor. Color is appropriate for ethnicity with no pallor, cyanosis, or jaundice. Hair distribution is consistent with age-appropriate sexual maturity rating (Tanner staging), including the development of axillary, pubic, and facial hair. Increased sebaceous gland activity may result in oilier skin or mild physiological acne (comedones). Nails are smooth, pink, with capillary refill less than 2 seconds. No edema, masses, or lymphadenopathy palpable.
results.female_result: Skin is warm, dry, and intact with good turgor. Color is appropriate for ethnicity with no pallor, cyanosis, or jaundice. results.male_result: Fasting insulin and C-peptide levels are significantly influenced by pubertal development (Tanner staging). During puberty (particularly mid-puberty), levels are naturally higher than in adults. Fasting insulin: 4.0-30.0 μIU/mL (higher range than adults); Fasting C-peptide: 0.9-4.5 ng/mL. Post-glucose load (1.75g/kg body weight, max 75g): Insulin secretion is hyper-responsive. Peak insulin at 30-60 min is often significantly higher than adults (can exceed 150-200 μIU/mL) to compensate for physiological insulin resistance. C-peptide peaks are similarly elevated. Levels should trend back towards baseline by 180 minutes.

results.male_result: Values are strictly dependent on pubertal development (Tanner Stages I-V). Testosterone (Total): Tanner I <30 ng/dL, Tanner II 18-150 ng/dL, Tanner III 100-320 ng/dL, Tanner IV 200-970 ng/dL, Tanner V 270-1070 ng/dL (reaches adult levels). FSH: Increases from 0.2 mIU/mL (prepubertal) to 1.5-12.4 mIU/mL. LH: Increases from 0.1 mIU/mL (prepubertal) to 1.7-8.6 mIU/mL. DHEA-S: Increases with age (Adrenarche), approx. 25-500 μg/dL. SHBG: Decreases during puberty from childhood highs (approx. 40-150 nmol/L) to adult levels.
results.female_result: Values are strictly dependent on pubertal development (Tanner Stages I-V) and menarche status. Estradiol: Tanner I <10-15 pg/mL, increasing progressively to adult levels (20-400 pg/mL) as cycles establish. FSH: Increases from <4.0 mIU/mL (prepubertal) to adult ranges. LH: Increases from <1.0 mIU/mL (prepubertal) to adult ranges; pulsatility begins in sleep during puberty. Progesterone: Remains low (<1.0 ng/mL) until ovulation begins (often irregular in first 2 years post-menarche). Testosterone: Increases slightly from <10 ng/dL to 15-70 ng/dL. DHEA-S: Increases with age, approx. 20-430 μg/dL.

results.male_result: HLA typing results are reported as specific allele designations for each locus. Normal finding: Successful identification of HLA alleles at Class I (HLA-A, HLA-B, HLA-C) and Class II (HLA-DR, HLA-DQ, HLA-DP) loci. Results expressed in standard nomenclature (e.g., HLA-A*02:01, HLA-B*07:02, HLA-DRB1*15:01). Each individual inherits one allele from each parent, resulting in heterozygous or homozygous expression at each locus. No 'normal' or 'abnormal' alleles exist; results represent individual genetic polymorphism.

results.male_result: Normal ICGA findings proceed through three distinct phases: 1. Early Phase (0-3 minutes): Rapid sequential filling of choroidal arteries, followed by the choriocapillaris flush, and finally choroidal veins. Large choroidal vessels appear hyperfluorescent (bright) as they fill with dye. 2. Mid Phase (3-15 minutes): Vessel margins become less distinct as the dye diffuses, resulting in a diffuse hyperfluorescence of the choroidal tissue. 3. Late Phase (15-30 minutes): The characteristic 'inversion phenomenon' is observed. Large choroidal vessels appear hypofluorescent (dark) silhouetted against a hyperfluorescent (bright) background caused by the staining of the choroidal stroma. The optic disc typically appears hypofluorescent in the late phase.

results.male_result: Histological appearance is strictly dependent on the stage of pubertal development (Tanner Staging). Early Adolescence (Tanner II-III): Seminiferous tubules enlarge, lumen formation begins, and primary spermatocytes appear; mature spermatozoa are typically absent. Late Adolescence (Tanner V): Histology resembles the adult pattern with full spermatogenesis (presence of spermatogonia, spermatocytes, spermatids, and mature spermatozoa), intact basement membrane, and mature Leydig cells. Johnsen score should range from near 1 to 10 depending on maturity.

results.male_result: Semen parameters are highly dependent on pubertal development (Tanner stages). In early adolescence (1-3 years post-spermarche), volume, concentration, and motility are typically lower than adult standards. By late adolescence (approx. 17-19 years or Tanner stage 5), values should align with adult WHO standards: Volume: ≥1.5 mL; pH: 7.2-8.0; Sperm concentration: ≥15 million/mL; Total sperm count: ≥39 million per ejaculate; Total motility (PR + NP): ≥40%; Vitality: ≥58% live sperm; Morphology: ≥4% normal forms.
results.female_result: Not applicable

results.male_result: Epidermis: Normal stratified squamous epithelium with intact stratum corneum, stratum granulosum, stratum spinosum, and stratum basale; no dyskeratosis or atypia. Dermis: Normal collagen and elastic fiber architecture; no inflammatory infiltrates, granulomas, or abnormal deposits. Adnexal structures: Hair follicles, sebaceous glands, and eccrine glands appear normal in morphology and distribution. No fungal elements (hyphae, spores), parasites (mites, larvae), or pathogenic microorganisms identified. Melanocytes present in normal distribution at the dermal-epidermal junction. No evidence of acantholysis, spongiosis, or interface dermatitis.
